heroHeadline: FIFA Council Approves Record $871M Payout; New Red Card Rules for Mouth-Covering and Walkouts; Iran Missing From Vancouver Due to Visa Crisis — 43 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: The FIFA Council met in Vancouver on April 29 and approved a record $871 million total financial contribution for the 2026 World Cup — a 15% increase from the original $727 million figure — after multiple UEFA member associations warned that competing on US soil would result in net financial losses despite the record prize pot. Preparation fees rose from $1.5M to $2.5M per team and qualification bonuses increased to $10M. In a parallel vote, IFAB unanimously approved two new red card laws for the tournament: players covering their mouths during confrontations with opponents may be dismissed (prompted by the Benfica/Prestianni–Vinicius Jr. Champions League incident), and players who walk off the pitch to protest a refereeing decision face immediate red cards, with teams potentially forfeiting the match (prompted by Senegal's 2025 AFCON final walkout). The Iran participation crisis deepened symbolically when Iranian federation delegates were absent from the Council meeting itself due to visa difficulties — directly foreshadowing the obstacles the squad will face entering the United States. The May 1 FIFA Congress remains the formal decision point on Iran's status. Separately, Vancouver Police denied FIFA's request for a presidential-level motorcade for Gianni Infantino, ruling he does not qualify as an Internationally Protected Person; FIFA distanced Infantino from the request. Mohamed Salah was confirmed injured for the rest of the Liverpool season after a hamstring injury on April 26 — Egypt project him fit for the June 15 opener vs Belgium. Germany delayed its squad announcement to May 21 due to injuries. Preliminary squads (35 players) are due May 11; final rosters (26 players) due June 1.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune, UPI, CNN, CNBC, NBC Dallas-Fort Worth, Fox 5 New York, Inside World Football, CBC Sports, Johnson County Post, PR Newswire, Yahoo Sports, RTE Sport, TRT World, Time, Xinhua, The National, HuffPost, Washington Post, Washington Times, Canada Soccer, DFB (German Football Association), beIN Sports, France 24, Globe and Mail, Global News, Africa Top Sports, Click2Houston, Republic World
